title: Human Trafficking Hotlines - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Crisis Hotlines > Human Trafficking Hotlines 7 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AT:PROJECT ANTI-TRAFFICKING ALLIANCE OF CENTRAL OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Anti-Trafficking Project and Cannabis Workers Program provides human and labor trafficking hotline services, including safety planning, risk assessments, hygiene items, clothing, food, mental health, substance abuse treatment referrals, applying for public assistance benefits, and document replacement. In addition, the program offers assistance with GED, resume building, transportation to interviews, and referrals to local services for job training and career development. Case management and medical accompaniment services are also available. All services are confidential. Eligibility: Anti-Trafficking Project: Individuals who are experiencing or survivors of sex or labor trafficking or exploitation Youth who have experience Commercial Sexual Exploitation of Children (CSEC). Cannabis Workers Program: Individuals who have worked in the marijuana industry who have experienced wage loss, unsafe living and working conditions, violence, labor exploitation, or human trafficking. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 647-0658 Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY 24-HOUR CRISIS LINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line that includes safety planning. 605 Center Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual abuse, human trafficking, and/or stalking, regardless of gender identity or sexual orientation Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 399-7722 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY WORKS HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis hotline and referral services about domestic violence, sexual assault, sex trafficking, homeless/runaway youth, and stalking. 2594 E Barnett Road Suite C Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 779-4357 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, protective order assistance, emergency shelter, safety planning, court and medical accompaniment, referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 726 N 1st Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, dating violence, human trafficking, and elder abuse. Also serves individulas with disabilities who are victims of abuse. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 947-2449 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HENDERSON HOUSE HENDERSON HOUSE ADVOCACY CENTER CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic violence and sexual assault client advocacy services. Services include a crisis hotline, coordinated wraparound service, referrals, assistance with filling out protective orders, youth outreach support groups, weekly support groups for domestic violence and sexual violence survivors (including adult survivors of child sexual abuse), and an art therapy group. 610 SE 1st Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Survivors of interpersonal and/or sexual violence (including stalking), human trafficking, and their children who have experienced or witnessed abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Support groups: Tuesday/ Thursday 6pm-7:30pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 472-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW DAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Supports the safety, needs, and rights of people experiencing sex trafficking or exploitation, trading sex, or who are at risk. Provides case outreach, case management/advocacy, and counseling. Helps with safety planning, transportation, and access to shelter or other housing resources. Also offers referral to additional resources, access to employment and education programs, and LGBTQ2+ resources. 314 SW 9th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 12-25 who have been impacted by sex trafficking Hours: Referral Line: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-7081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLARIS NATIONAL HUMAN TRAFFICKING HOTLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a national, toll-free hotline available to answer calls, texts, and chats serving victims and survivors of human trafficking and the anti-trafficking community. Also provides a tip line for reports of potential human trafficking. The hotline is a non-profit, non-governmental organization working exclusively on the issue of human trafficking and is not associated with law enforcement or immigration authorities. Eligibility: Hotline: Individuals who are compelled to engage in commercial sex through force, fraud, or coercion OR under 18 and engaging in commercial sex, regardless of whether force, fraud, or coercion is also taking place Tip Line: Unrestricted Hours: Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 373-7888 24 7 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY 24-HOUR CRISIS LINE COMMUNITY WORKS HELPLINE DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER HENDERSON HOUSE CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH NEW DAY
